text: Moving from the quiet formality of a nearly empty seminar room to the energetic creativity of an exhibition space, I feel a sense of anticipation mixed with vulnerability. In the first scene, the arrangement of chairs and the faint presence of others invites reflection—perhaps on knowledge, performance, or the expectations we carry into communal spaces. The second scene swells with color and concepts: "R+D"—a call to question, innovate, and, beneath the surface, a subtle exploration of misunderstanding and connection. I identify with the words on the screen, recognizing my own desire to bridge the gulf between intention and perception. Here, I am both spectator and participant, aware of how spaces—physical and intellectual—shape how I see myself and others.
